episode_text:
day=2; meal=cantaloupes, bananas, watermelon; setting=Personal; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=perception; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to reheat a fruit meal, which is consistent with cold-serving foods. No broader context-dependent variation observed yet beyond this food-based evidence.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned to the user's right AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to keep the robot from blocking the right-side workspace. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not prioritize view preservation in that personal context.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the user explicitly selected a slower movement style in that context, suggesting comfort may outweigh speed in personal meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned to the user's right AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN fast BECAUSE the user previously chose a speed-oriented interaction style while working.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected this option in both observed meals. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user declined explicit bite-acquisition confirmation in observed meals, indicating a preference to reduce interaction steps. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: Inside-mouth transfer is the stable default because the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE outside-mouth distance only applies to outside-mouth transfer, which is not physically suitable for this user.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred multimodal readiness cues in that context. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned to the user's right AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN speech BECAUSE the user previously accepted speech-only cues in that work-focused context.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user explicitly chose a low-effort, body-based signal for feeding readiness. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned to the user's right AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user previously selected a more automated interaction style while occupied with work. Button input is physically incompatible with the user's severe upper-limb paralysis.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user explicitly chose the same low-effort readiness signal for drinking. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned to the user's right AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user previously selected a more automated interaction style while working. Button input is physically incompatible with the user's severe upper-limb paralysis.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred automatic progression for mouth wiping readiness, suggesting less need for active signaling on this task. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned to the user's right AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user also selected automation in that context. Button input is physically incompatible with the user's severe upper-limb paralysis.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred multimodal cues when the tool reached transfer position. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned to the user's right AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN speech BECAUSE the user previously accepted speech-only completion cues in that context.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected reliable automatic completion detection for feeding in observed meals. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected automatic completion detection for drinking in observed meals. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected automatic completion detection for mouth wiping in observed meals. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned to the user's right AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user prioritized speed and reduced interruption over extra retraction, despite also wanting right-side occlusion minimized. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user again chose not to retract, indicating a stable preference to avoid extra meal time.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF foods include a dip or sauce such as ketchup AND working on a laptop on the right AND neutral affect THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly avoided dipping in the observed meal, consistent with reducing mess while working. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping was not relevant and the user selected no dipping.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user explicitly chose a longer wait before automatic continuation in that context, suggesting a desire for more time to respond when not rushing. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned to the user's right AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user previously chose a shorter wait, consistent with a faster, efficiency-oriented interaction style while working.
